name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
short_description: Repealed parts of Glass-Steagall; allowed commercial/investment bank mergers.
notes: Later criticized after 2008 financial crisis; bipartisan at signing (362-57 House).

name: USA PATRIOT Act
short_description: Expanded surveillance authorities post-9/11.
notes: 79% initial public support per Pew. Approval eroded over time as civil-liberties concerns surfaced.

name: No Child Left Behind Act
short_description: Federal standards-based education reform with testing requirements.
notes: Broad bipartisan support at signing; became controversial over 'teaching to the test' complaints.

name: Iraq War Resolution (AUMF)
short_description: Authorized use of force against Iraq.
notes: Approved 77-23 Senate. Public support eroded sharply by 2006.

name: Medicare Modernization Act
short_description: Added Medicare Part D prescription-drug benefit.
notes: Controversial at passage; approval grew as program proved popular.

name: Emergency Economic Stabilization Act (TARP)
short_description: $700B financial-sector bailout during 2008 crisis.
notes: Deeply unpopular with public (~40% approved) despite economic necessity consensus.

name: American Recovery and Reinvestment Act
short_description: $787B stimulus package in wake of Great Recession.
notes: Narrow approval at signing; retrospective views improved as recession ended.

name: Affordable Care Act
short_description: Expanded health insurance coverage via exchanges, Medicaid expansion, mandates.
notes: Polling split 45-50 at signing; approval flipped positive by late 2010s.

name: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform
short_description: Major post-2008 financial-system reform; created CFPB.
notes: Mildly positive approval; partially rolled back 2018.

name: JOBS Act
short_description: Loosened securities regulations for startups and IPOs.
notes: Strong bipartisan support; 380-41 House.

name: Every Student Succeeds Act
short_description: Replaced NCLB; returned more authority to states.
notes: Bipartisan consensus at signing.

name: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act
short_description: Major corporate + individual tax overhaul.
notes: Polling 32-50 at signing. Approval remained mixed through enactment.

name: First Step Act
short_description: Criminal-justice reform; sentence reductions.
notes: Rare bipartisan win; 87-12 Senate.

name: CARES Act
short_description: $2.2T COVID-19 relief; direct payments, PPP, unemployment expansion.
notes: Near-universal support (96-0 Senate). Peak approval 70%+ initially.

name: American Rescue Plan Act
short_description: $1.9T COVID relief + stimulus round 2.
notes: 50-50 Senate (Harris tiebreaker); approval 60% initial, eroded on inflation concerns.

name: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act
short_description: $1.2T infrastructure package.
notes: Bipartisan 69-30 Senate. Generally positive approval.

name: CHIPS and Science Act
short_description: $280B semiconductor / science funding.
notes: Strong bipartisan support; strategic-industry investment.

name: Inflation Reduction Act
short_description: Climate, health, tax changes; IRS funding.
notes: Party-line Senate. Polling 37-42 on passage.

name: Respect for Marriage Act
short_description: Federal recognition of same-sex and interracial marriage.
notes: Bipartisan (61-36 Senate). Broadly popular (~70% public approval).

name: Fiscal Responsibility Act
short_description: Debt-ceiling deal with spending caps.
notes: Avoided default; bipartisan. Mixed public reception.
